name: QAnon Full
description: QAnon is a far-right conspiracy theory originating in late 2017. It centers on the baseless claim that a cabal of Satan-worshipping, cannibalistic pedophiles secretly runs a global child sex trafficking ring and plotted against former U.S. President Donald Trump during his term in office. QAnon believers claim that Trump was secretly fighting this cabal and that a 'storm' of arrests and executions is coming for its members, who include prominent Democrats, Hollywood elites, and other figures. The theory originated with posts by 'Q,' an anonymous individual or group claiming to be a high-ranking government official with access to classified information, on the imageboard 4chan. These posts, often cryptic and vague, are interpreted by followers as clues or prophecies about future events. Proponents cite supposed 'evidence' in Q's posts, perceived inconsistencies in official narratives, and interpretations of current events to support their beliefs. Mainstream scholarship and journalism widely dismiss QAnon as a dangerous and unfounded conspiracy theory. It has been linked to real-world violence, including the January 6th Capitol attack, and has been identified as a significant source of disinformation and political polarization. Psychological explanations for QAnon's appeal include a desire for simple explanations in a complex world, a sense of belonging within a community, and confirmation bias. The theory has evolved and adapted over time, incorporating elements of other conspiracy theories and spreading globally through social media.
subtitle: Far-right conspiracy theory about a secret cabal.

claims.claim: A cabal of Satan-worshipping pedophiles controls the world.
claims.evidence: Cryptic posts by 'Q,' interpreted to suggest hidden symbols and messages pointing to the existence of the cabal.
claims.criticism: No credible evidence supports the existence of such a cabal. The claims are based on unsubstantiated rumors and distorted interpretations of events.

claims.claim: Donald Trump was secretly fighting against the cabal.
claims.evidence: Q's posts often praised Trump and suggested he was working to expose the cabal. Events during Trump's presidency are interpreted as strategic moves in this secret war.
claims.criticism: Trump's actions and statements do not align with the purported fight against a pedophile cabal. There is no evidence that Trump was aware of or involved in any such operation.

claims.claim: A 'storm' of arrests and executions is coming for the cabal members.
claims.evidence: Q often alluded to a future reckoning, referred to as 'the storm,' where the cabal members would be brought to justice.
claims.criticism: No such arrests or executions have occurred, and there is no evidence to suggest they will. The 'storm' is a baseless prophecy with no basis in reality.

keyFigures.name: Travis View
keyFigures.role: Journalist and researcher critical of QAnon

timeline.event: QAnon gains popularity and spreads through social media
QAnon believers participate in the U.S. Capitol attack

sources.author: Mike Rothschild
sources.work: The Storm Is Upon Us: How QAnon Became a Movement, Cult, and Conspiracy Theory of Everything
sources.title: Mike Rothschild
